title: Access Review Policy
body:
# Access Review Policy

## Purpose
This policy establishes requirements for periodic review of user access rights to ensure that only authorized individuals have access to corporate systems and data.

## Scope
This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and third-party users who have been granted access to any corporate information system.

## Policy
1. **Annual Review**: All user access rights must be reviewed at least once per calendar year. The review shall verify that each user’s access permissions are appropriate for their current role and responsibilities.
2. **Access Revocation**: Access that is no longer required must be revoked promptly. Managers must notify IT within 5 business days of a role change or termination.
3. **Documentation**: All access reviews must be documented and records retained for a minimum of 3 years.
4. **Segregation of Duties**: User access must be assigned in a manner that maintains appropriate segregation of duties to prevent conflicts of interest or fraud.

## Compliance
Non-compliance with this policy may result in disciplinary action, up to and including termination of employment or contract.

title: Data Retention Policy
body:
# Data Retention Policy

## Purpose
This policy defines how long different categories of data must be retained to meet legal, regulatory, and business requirements.

## Scope
All corporate data, whether stored on-premises or in cloud environments, is covered by this policy.

## Retention Schedules
- **Financial Records**: 7 years from the end of the fiscal year.
- **HR Records**: 10 years after termination of employment.
- **Operational Logs**: 1 year from creation.
- **Customer Contracts**: 5 years after contract termination.

## Disposal
Data that has exceeded its retention period must be securely destroyed in accordance with the Data Disposal Procedure.
